headline: How The Jet2 (AIM:JET2) Investment Narrative Is Shifting As Analyst Targets Tighten
summary: Jet2’s latest analyst updates centre on price targets drifting from £14.50 toward a tighter £13.25 to £14.00 band, with fair value estimates trimmed from £17.76 to £17.32. JPMorgan and others have kept broadly Neutral or Equal Weight stances while adjusting these targets, which signals fine tuning around expectations rather than a wholesale rethink of the story. As you read on, you will see how these shifting targets feed into the evolving Jet2 narrative and what to watch next. Analyst Price...

headline: Private Credit Funding Strain Tests Hamilton Lane’s Evolving Growth Story
summary: JPMorgan Chase has started restricting lending to private credit providers, reflecting increased caution around the health of the private credit sector. These tighter funding conditions have raised liquidity concerns and contributed to redemption limits at several large private credit players. Hamilton Lane (NasdaqGS:HLNE), which has significant exposure to private credit, has seen its share price come under pressure as investors reassess risk. The market reaction highlights growing concern...

headline: Are private credit troubles here to stay as firms cap withdrawals?
summary: Investors are growing increasingly concerned about the state of the private credit industry as Morgan Stanley (MS) and BlackRock (BLK) join the ranks of firms restricting withdrawals from their funds. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Bullseye American Ingenuity Fund portfolio manager Adam Johnson discuss the themes of uncertainty emerging from the private credit industry. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Morning Brief.

headline: Lazard Faces Redemption Limits As Private Credit Risks Hit Valuation
summary: Lazard, along with several major financial firms, has limited redemptions for certain funds following JPMorgan's move to restrict lending to private credit providers. The actions reflect industry wide caution about liquidity and the valuation of private credit loans. These restrictions have been introduced as concerns build around potential stress points in the private credit market. Lazard, traded as NYSE:LAZ, sits in the middle of this tension between investor demand for higher yielding...

headline: Adobe Could Reach $420 by Year-End as JPMorgan Sees a Buying Opportunity

headline: Higher Oil Prices Could Bring a Global Economic Shock. This Is the Number to Watch.
summary: Oil prices are key to watch.  If they rise above $125 a barrel and stay there, it would create the type of economic ripples seen in other shocks, according to  JP Morgan  Chief Economist Bruce Kasman.  In the past five energy shocks, the last of which was when Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022, Brent has soared 50% above its trailing two-year average, according to Kasman.

content.title: Higher Oil Prices Could Bring a Global Economic Shock. This Is the Number to Watch.
content.summary: Oil prices are key to watch.  If they rise above $125 a barrel and stay there, it would create the type of economic ripples seen in other shocks, according to  JP Morgan  Chief Economist Bruce Kasman.  In the past five energy shocks, the last of which was when Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022, Brent has soared 50% above its trailing two-year average, according to Kasman.

headline: Concorde Financial Exits Howard Hughes Holdings, Developer of Large Sun Belt Communities
summary: Howard Hughes owns large land holdings in fast-growing markets such as Las Vegas, Houston, and Phoenix, where it has developed master-planned communities over many years. Investors will watch whether the company can convert housing demand into both land sales and long-term retail, office, and mixed-use development.

headline: JPMorgan, Partners Group drop UAE events as war drags on
summary: Partners Group has moved its annual global gathering of investors to Switzerland from Abu Dhabi, people familiar with the matter said, declining to be identified discussing the matter.  JPMorgan is re-scheduling its MENA Global Opportunities Conference, an invitation-only event for institutional investors, corporate executives, and financial sponsors, other people said.
